metadata.title:
PR0SPERS IN SPITE


OF LAUNDRY TRUST

pages.raw_text:
While in Seattle recently attend
ing the meeting of the state feder-
alton of labor, James Stevenson
purchased more equipment for the
local union laundry In addressing
the Tacoma Trades council Mr..
Stevenson stated that the unions
had now invested in the plant over
$4,000, and the business and good
will of the plant was easily worth
100000. He said the local laundry
trust had thrown every obstacle in
the way Of the union project, and
recited the difficulties the union
men had to overcome to secure ma
chinery and supplies. 1t has been
necessary to buy their machinery
in Seattle, one firm in that city be-
ing disposed to sell machinery to
the local plant despite the protests
of the laundrymen of the state.
